Agent reliability: timeout, /continue, multi-agent MCP fix

- Default agent timeout 10min → 30min, add per-agent --timeout flag
  (/agent --timeout 1h <prompt>)
- Add /continue command to resume finished/timed-out agents by label

  works for follow-ups

  and efficiency guidance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AMQPRPCClient for RPC-style messaging with direct reply-to (#191)

* Add RPC client and server with session integration

Add AMQPRPCClient (direct reply-to) and AMQPRPCServer (queue-based)
for request-response messaging. Both integrate with AMQPSession:

- session.rpcClient() creates a reusable client tracked for reconnect recovery
- session.rpcServer(queue, handler) creates a server using session queue subscribe
- session.rpcCall(queue, body) does a one-shot RPC call

RPC clients get automatic recovery on reconnect. RPC servers recover
through the existing queue/subscription machinery. session.stop()
cleans up everything.

* Address review feedback: simplify RPC handler, remove oneshot rpcCall

- Simplify RPCHandler signature from (body, msg) to just (msg)
- Remove session.rpcCall() one-shot convenience method
- Add comment explaining unmatched correlationId behavior in RPC client
- Flatten nested if-statements in RPC client consumer callback
- Update README, CHANGELOG, and all tests

https://claude.ai/code/session_016fEPinum7XFHdDBqX54iAn

* Restore rpcCall as the recommended high-level RPC API

rpcCall is the simplest entry point for most users. rpcClient is the
optimization for high-throughput scenarios that need to avoid per-call
channel overhead. Updated docs and comments to reflect this.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016fEPinum7XFHdDBqX54iAn

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: skip active bullet when searching for drag parent to prevent self-parenting

When dragging a bullet that immediately precedes its drop target, the parent
search loop would find the active bullet itself as the parent candidate (same
depth), creating a cycle the server rejects. Skip the active bullet in the loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: wire Hebbian seed confidence into DSP cache gate + fix naive cache

DSP signal chain now receives real cache_confidence from HebbianSynaptic:
- Buddhi.pre_flight(seed=) looks up per-seed Hebbian weight
- High confidence seeds → DSP cache gate attenuates budget (agent responds concisely)
- Continuous: gain *= (1.0 - confidence * 0.5), not a hard switch

Cache hardening (was dangerously naive):
- Cache HIT requires Hebbian confidence > 0.7 (~5 successful uses of same seed)
- Cache STORE requires confidence > 0.7 (no first-success caching)
- Short TTL: 60 seconds (agent context changes fast, stale cache is toxic)
- Hebbian learning runs BEFORE cache store (learn first, then decide to cache)
- First-success caching eliminated — the response might have been mediocre

The old cache was a tape recorder. The new cache is confidence-gated:
seed seen 1x → no cache. seed seen 5x successfully → cache with 60s TTL.

859 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: multi-line body parsing in PR validation hook (#911)

fix(git-plugin): fix multi-line PR body parsing in issue link hook

The validate-pr-issue-links hook used perl -ne (line-by-line processing)
to extract --body content. Multi-line PR bodies had the opening quote on
one line and closing quote on another, so the regex never matched. The
hook always reported "missing issue closing keywords" even when they
were present in the body.

Fix: use perl -0777 to slurp entire input as a single string.

Add regression test covering single-line, multi-line, and heredoc body
formats.

commit.message:
fix: bypass chi Compress middleware for MCP SSE to enable streaming

Chi's Compress middleware wraps the ResponseWriter even when Accept-Encoding
is stripped, which swallows http.Flusher.Flush() calls and buffers SSE events
until connection close. Replace skipCompressForSSE with wrapCompressSkipMCP
that completely bypasses the Compress middleware for /mcp/ paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Docker deployment support (#276)

* feat: add Docker deployment support

* feat: add docker-compose.yml

* feat: add .dockerignore

* feat: add docker entrypoint

* feat: add Docker deployment docs

* fix: correct Docker setup for zylos-core installation and container networking

- Use Node 20 (LTS) instead of Node 24
- Fix zylos-core install: npm from GitHub instead of non-existent npm registry
- Set up non-root user npm global prefix for proper zylos CLI access
- Fix init flags: use --yes (not --no-interactive), pass auth credentials
- Add WEB_CONSOLE_BIND=0.0.0.0 for container port accessibility
- Add claude-config volume to persist Claude Code auth across restarts
- Remove deprecated docker-compose version field
- Update docs with new volume and env var

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify Docker entrypoint — delegate .env to zylos init

- Remove manual .env generation from entrypoint; zylos init handles it
- Pass auth via env vars (init reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN)
- Pass TZ via --timezone flag (init ignores ambient TZ by design)
- Channel tokens (Telegram, Lark) appended via upsert after init
- Replace 4 named volumes with single zylos-data volume for ~/zylos
- Keep claude-config volume separate for ~/.claude
- Add /usr/local/bin to PATH in Dockerfile
- Update docs: remove cp .env.example step, add "How It Works" section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove init marker — run zylos init on every startup

Ensures template files (skills, PM2 config) stay in sync after
docker image upgrades without manual intervention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update entrypoint header comment to reflect every-startup init

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add local build instructions to Docker guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove redundant Claude Code install, fix ro mount bug, fix docs

- Remove manual Claude Code install from Dockerfile (zylos init handles it)
- Add write protection to upsert_env for read-only mounted .env
- Move "That's it" paragraph before "Building locally" section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: increase healthcheck start_period to 10 minutes

First boot may take several minutes (zylos init + Claude Code install).
Docker marks healthy as soon as the first check passes, so the longer
grace period has no downside.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-detect auth token type, enable local build, improve init errors

- Entrypoint auto-detects sk-ant-oat (setup token) vs sk-ant (API key)
  regardless of which env var it's set in. Prevents misrouted auth.
- docker-compose.yml: enable build alongside image so --build works
- Entrypoint: warn on init failure instead of silent || true
- Docs: use --build in quick start, remove separate build section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: prioritize CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN over ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

Swap auth token ordering in Quick Start, env table, and .env.example
to reflect that OAuth token is the recommended default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback — SIGTERM trap, env override, healthcheck

1. Add trap cleanup (SIGTERM/SIGINT) in entrypoint.sh for graceful
   shutdown of tmux and PM2 on docker stop
2. Change upsert_env to update existing values — env vars from
   docker-compose now take precedence over stale .env entries
3. Remove duplicate HEALTHCHECK from Dockerfile (compose defines
   the correct start_period=600s)

Addresses zylos0t review items 1, 2, and 4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add Docker quick start section to README

Adds a collapsible Docker deployment section in Quick Start with
a link to the full Docker guide (docs/docker.md).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: no-clone Docker deployment — docker run + curl compose

- README: Docker quick start is now a single `docker run` command
- docs/docker.md: Two paths — docker run (simplest) and docker compose
  (curl download, no repo clone required)
- docker-compose.yml: comment out `build: .` so standalone download
  works without Dockerfile; swap auth token order

Follows the pattern used by Open WebUI, Immich, and other popular
open-source projects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify claude-config volume purpose

Explain that claude-config persists Claude Code login state across
restarts. Note it's optional since entrypoint re-configures auth,
but recommended.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: upgrade base image to node:22-slim (current LTS)

Node 20 EOL is 2026-04. Node 22 LTS is supported until 2027-04.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix html element scrolling away the header

CSS spec: when body has overflow:hidden, the browser transfers the
scroll container to <html>. Since html had no overflow:hidden, the
entire page (including the header) could scroll off-screen when the
user tried to scroll. Adding overflow:hidden to html prevents this.

fix: restore dnd-kit drag by merging pointer handlers on bullet dot

The spread listeners.onPointerDown was being overwritten by the explicit
onPointerDown prop, so dnd-kit never received the event and drag never started.
Extract dnd-kit's onPointerDown and call both handlers together.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[Model Monitoring] Optimize TimescaleDB storage for app_results table (#9237)

## Summary
Reduce storage overhead in TimescaleDB model monitoring tables through
index removal, data type optimization, and empty JSON handling.

**Investigation findings from vmdev37:**
- 18.7M rows across 2 app_results tables (4000 endpoints × 12 app/result
combos × 15-min intervals)
- Storage: 2.6 GB data + 1.1 GB indexes = 3.7 GB total

## Changes Made
- Remove unused indexes from `app_results`, `metrics`, and `errors`
tables (0 scans in production per `pg_stat_user_indexes` analysis)
- Change `result_status` and `result_kind` from INTEGER to SMALLINT
(values only 0-2, saves 2 bytes/row each)
- Store NULL instead of `"{}"` for empty `result_extra_data` (saves 6
bytes/row)
- Add backwards compatibility: convert NULL back to `"{}"` on read via
`fillna()`

## Testing
- All 113 TimescaleDB unit tests pass
- Verified backwards compatibility with NULL → "{}" conversion

## Impact
- **Estimated savings: ~487 MB (18%)** for 18.6M rows
- No breaking changes - backwards compatible with existing data
- New tables will use optimized schema; existing tables unaffected until
recreation

## Reference
- Jira: [ML-11777](https://iguazio.atlassian.net/browse/ML-11777)

[ML-11777]:
https://iguazio.atlassian.net/browse/ML-11777?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiNWRkNTljNzYxNjVmNDY3MDlhMDU5Y2ZhYzA5YTRkZjUiLCJwIjoiZ2l0aHViLWNvbS1KU1cifQ

commit.message:
feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff (#7)

* feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff

Implement TypeORM-style auto-generation that compares current database
state with Drizzle entity definitions and generates migrations automatically.

Core Features:
- Schema introspection for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite
- Schema loading from Drizzle entity files (supports multi-directory)
- Schema diffing engine to detect tables, columns, indexes, and FK changes
- SQL generation with dialect-specific DDL statements
- Auto-generated up() and down() migration methods
- Fallback to blank template when no schema changes detected

New Components:
- SchemaIntrospector: Read current database schema metadata (412 lines)
- SchemaLoader: Parse Drizzle table definitions from TS files (228 lines)
- SchemaDiffer: Compare schemas and detect changes (227 lines)
- SqlGenerator: Generate dialect-specific DDL (360 lines)

Configuration:
- Extended MigrationConfig with schemaFiles and dbCredentials
- MigrationGenerator now async to support database introspection
- Backward compatible with existing blank migration workflow

Testing:
- Added 46 unit tests (schema-differ, sql-generator)
- Added 53 integration tests (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite)
- All 99 tests passing with real database connections

Dependencies:
- Added drizzle-kit@^0.30.0 for schema parsing
- Added better-sqlite3@^11.0.0 for SQLite testing

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Resolve ESLint errors and warnings

- Fix case block lexical declaration error in generator.ts
- Remove unused imports (TableIndex, ForeignKey) from sql-generator.ts
- Prefix unused variable with underscore in schema-loader.ts
- Auto-fix prettier/prettier formatting issues

Reduced lint issues from 65 to 43 (0 errors, 43 warnings).
Remaining warnings are acceptable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
warnings for dynamic database result handling.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

All files now pass prettier --check validation.
Tests verified: 46 unit tests passing.

these updates add prominence to the new feature at the top of the file.

repository.name: drizzle-tx-migrations
repository.description: TypeORM-like migrations for Drizzle ORM with transaction support

commit.message:
Rename version folders to v1.0/v2.0 format and update JSON version fields

- Renamed all version folders from v1 to v1.0, v2 to v2.0
- Updated version field in all module JSONs to match (v1.0, v2.0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update E2E test plan with 12 new single-statement architecture scenarios

- Add condition 7 (signal qualification) to quick reference table
- Add concept reference for new architecture (qualification window, frozen
  snapshot, effective rank, tracking status, ranking eligibility)
- Update existing scenarios 2-5 with new qualification expectations
- Add scenarios 30-41: qualification window (AT_OPEN, WITHIN_WINDOW, expired),
  frozen snapshot immutability, 3-block statement UI, effective rank formula,
  tracking status lifecycle, ranking eligibility, equity drawdown, risk money
  backfill, statement API
- Add 6 new launch-blocking bugs for new features
- Update final reconciliation checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
